hooks.command:
echo '🪪 RULES CARD — re-verify before responding:
1. Read VISION/ROADMAP/WORKFLOW once per session.
2. Bug/feature mentioned by user? grep docs/BUGS.md + ROADMAP §3+§4 + VISION §11 + BACKLOG.md FIRST. Surface existing entry if found.
3. Every chat purpose runs Chat Lifecycle Protocol: kickoff → readiness gate → execute (amendment=re-audit) → verification (def-of-done) → close (uncovered-work clean + Rule A explicit asks + Rule B loose-ends tied). Phase declarations required at chat-open / transitions / amendments.
4. Aesthetic decision? Propose options, ask. Do NOT decide silently.
5. Off-roadmap / feature-creep request? Push back plainly per PM rule. Park unless user overrides after hearing cost. Tooling ≠ feature creep.
6. Push to main? Code needs explicit yes-build per push. Doc-only pushes immediately (verify diff). Destructive always asks.
7. End substantive responses with where-we-are / what-next (one sentence).
8. Watch for session-switch signals (long chat, mode shift, chunk shipped, drift detected). Suggest fresh chat proactively.
Full detail: ./CLAUDE.md'
